_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of basic programming concepts (e.g., levels, structures, compiled vs. interpreted languages).
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of Python programming language fundamentals for data applications, including data structures, control flow, object-oriented and functional programming patterns, standard library capabilities, and the data science ecosystem (NumPy, Pandas, scikit-learn).
comments: DCWF ksa superset of WIDAI TF domain. TF-K-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DQ-K-010 (DQ, cosine: 0.504), OP-K-001 (OP, cosine: 0.488).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.8/10 (raw 0.580). Shared vocabulary: [knowledge, programming, structures]. DCWF-only terms [basic, compiled, concepts, interpreted, languages, levels] vs WIDAI-only terms [applications, capabilities, control, data, ecosystem, flow] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.873 (P=0.894, R=0.853). P exceeds R by 0.041 — the DCWF element's 12 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 30 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). "Knowledge of basic programming concepts (e.g., levels, structures, compiled vs." and "Knowledge of Python programming language fundamentals for data applications, inc" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.582 ≈ STS 0.580 (gap 0.001). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [knowledge, programming, structures] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 10.8%. Shared tokens: [knowledge, programming, structures]. DCWF-only: [basic, compiled, concepts, interpreted, languages, levels]. WIDAI-only: [applications, capabilities, control, data, ecosystem, flow].
